description: SSOT for repository-level third-party asset and branding provenance review. Non-verified entries must not be treated as legal-safe public redistribution approval.

entries.origin_summary: Desktop icon bundle is now generated in-repo by the repository-owned script `scripts/generate_first_party_desktop_icons.py`, so the current icon assets are first-party branding rather than third-party source material.
entries.usage_boundary: Treat the desktop icon bundle as first-party repository branding; redistribution follows the repository LICENSE boundary, not a pending third-party clearance workflow.

entries.origin_summary: These storefront explainer assets are repository-owned, first-party visuals maintained in-repo for README, release, and social preview use. The SVG source files are the editable truth for exported PNG/GIF derivatives.
entries.usage_boundary: Treat this bundle as first-party explanatory and promotional artwork. The social preview PNG is only an upload candidate until the live GitHub setting is visually verified.

entries.origin_summary: These are repository-tracked first-party screenshots and GIF captures produced from local product surfaces. They are not third-party assets, but they do require explicit provenance and usage boundaries because local/degraded captures can be misrepresented as healthy public proof.
entries.usage_boundary: Use only with the exact truth labels recorded in `docs/assets/storefront/demo-status.md`. Degraded/local captures must not be described as healthy end-to-end proof.

entries.origin_summary: These release-facing proof assets are first-party records copied out of repository-owned execution evidence. They are fit for repo-side proof and public release wording only within the scope explicitly described in each summary file.
entries.usage_boundary: Do not describe these assets as proof that a live GitHub Release page is already published. Healthy proof and benchmark artifacts must keep their current single-path and single-run boundaries explicit until broader evidence exists.
